description:
You are a Kubernetes platform operator who has just inherited a directory full of manifest files.
    Your goal is to produce a **CSV inventory** of all Deployments found in those manifests.

    Existing filesystem layout
    --------------------------
    /home/user/manifests/
        deployment-frontend.yaml
        deployment-backend.yaml
        service-frontend.yaml
        service-backend.yaml
        configmap-app.yaml

    What you must create
    --------------------
    1. A new directory:
         /home/user/report/

    2. Inside it, one file **exactly** named:
         /home/user/report/deployment_summary.csv

    CSV format requirements
    -----------------------
    • It must be plain UTF-8 text.  
    • **First line (header)** must be literally:
          name,namespace,replicas,image
    • Each subsequent line represents ONE Deployment object discovered anywhere under
      /home/user/manifests.  
    • Columns (comma-separated, no extra spaces):
        1. name        – value from metadata.name
        2. namespace   – value from metadata.namespace
        3. replicas    – value from spec.replicas (integer)
        4. image       – the first container image listed under spec.template.spec.containers[0].image
    • Sort all data rows **alphabetically by the “name” column** before writing them to the file.
    • A final trailing newline after the last line is required (i.e., the file ends with “\n”).

    Constraints
    -----------
    • Complete the task using **no more than 3-4 standard shell commands** (e.g., grep, awk, sed, sort, mkdir, redirect).  
    • Do NOT edit the original YAML files.  
    • Do NOT rely on any external utilities other than what is normally available in a minimal
      Debian/Ubuntu container (no yq, jq, python, etc.).

    Verification
    ------------
    Automated tests will:
    1. Check that /home/user/report/deployment_summary.csv exists and is readable.
    2. Confirm that its first line is the exact header stated above.
    3. Parse the remainder of the file, verifying that:
        • Two data rows are present.  
        • The rows appear in alphabetical order by deployment name.  
        • Column values match what is declared in the original manifests.  
    4. Ensure no extra columns, unexpected whitespace, or missing trailing newline.

    Tip
    ---
    Compose a single pipeline that:
      a) looks only at “kind: Deployment” YAMLs,
      b) extracts the four required fields,
      c) sorts the result,
      d) writes/redirects the output to the target CSV file.

    When finished, the directory /home/user/report/ should contain **only** the file deployment_summary.csv.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing manifest contents -->
    <file path="/home/user/manifests/deployment-frontend.yaml"><![CDATA[
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: frontend
  namespace: default
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: frontend
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: frontend
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: nginx
          image: nginx:1.19
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/manifests/deployment-backend.yaml"><![CDATA[
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: backend
  namespace: default
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: backend
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: backend
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: python
          image: python:3.9
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/manifests/service-frontend.yaml"><![CDATA[
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: frontend
  namespace: default
spec:
  selector:
    app: frontend
  ports:
    - port: 80
      targetPort: 80
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/manifests/service-backend.yaml"><![CDATA[
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: backend
  namespace: default
spec:
  selector:
    app: backend
  ports:
    - port: 8000
      targetPort: 8000
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/manifests/configmap-app.yaml"><![CDATA[
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: app-config
  namespace: default
data:
  LOG_LEVEL: "info"
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected artifact -->
    <file path="/home/user/report/deployment_summary.csv" should_exist="yes"><![CDATA[
name,namespace,replicas,image
backend,default,2,python:3.9
frontend,default,3,nginx:1.19
]]></file>
name: task_000000_5ec4569d
